title: cynoglossum coeruleum subsp. johnstonii
intro: Cynoglossum coeruleum subsp. johnstonii, also known as Johnston's hound's tongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to East Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, derived from its leaves, flowers, and roots. The health benefits of Johnston's hound's tongue include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, coughs, and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a tea or by mixing the roots with other herbs to create a medicinal paste.
